canonical_truth.summary: Post-storm invertebrate emergence covers bark and dead wood surfaces across the territory — beetles, grubs, and centipedes surfaced by yesterday's rain in unusual numbers.
canonical_truth.location: forest-wide; root cross, shade corridor, east clearing
canonical_truth.concrete_outcome: abundant accessible food on branch surfaces without a ranging run; multiple animals feeding; YY eats well in the branches

perception_prompt.noticeable_surface: beetles, grubs, and pale centipedes moving on wet bark and exposed wood — more than the branches usually hold, all of them surfaced by yesterday's rain

authorial_note.real_world_inspiration: London Marathon 2026 — tens of thousands of runners moving through the city in a mass-participation surge
authorial_note.translation_logic: marathon's mass movement translates to post-storm invertebrate emergence — wet ground pushes creatures to bark and branch surfaces in unusual numbers, visible and accessible without a foraging run
authorial_note.restraint_reason: event enters through physical observation of bark surfaces, not through announcement; the food source is concrete and sensory; no mention of the real-world event needed in the prose
